title: Lipochaeta hastulata
intro: Lipochaeta hastulata, also known as the Hawaiian lipocheta, is a perennial herb native to the islands of Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Lipochaeta hastulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbal medicine, Lipochaeta hastulata is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it is sometimes used in combination with other local herbs to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Lipochaeta hastulata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Hawaii and is known for its yellow or white flowers. The classification of Lipochaeta hastulata is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, the clade Asterids, the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae, the subfamily Asteroideae, and the tribe Heliantheae. Within the tribe Heliantheae, Lipochaeta hastulata is placed in the genus Lipochaeta and is the type species. This classification helps to identify and organize the plant within the larger botanical system.
